family.reason: Beaches like Agia Anna and Pefki, calm atmosphere, easy access from Athens

couples.reason: Hidden coves in the south, romantic thermal springs in Edipsos, quiet mountain villages

food.reason: Local figs, mountain honey, fresh seafood, excellent mountain tavernas

beaches.reason: Diverse range: Chiliadou, Agia Anna, remote southern coves

walking.reason: Dirfys mountain hiking, Dimosari Gorge, forest paths

quick_summary.en: Greece's second-largest island—a land of wild mountains, thermal springs, and untouched beaches, offering a raw and authentic escape just a bridge-crossing from Athens.
quick_summary.el: Το δεύτερο μεγαλύτερο νησί της Ελλάδας—ένας τόπος με άγρια βουνά, ιαματικές πηγές και ανέγγιχτες παραλίες, προσφέροντας μια αυθεντική απόδραση μόλις μια γέφυρα μακριά από την Αθήνα.

pros: Diverse landscape—from lush pine forests in the north to rugged, arid beauty in the south
Easy access—connected to the mainland by bridge in Chalkida
Exceptional variety of beaches—organized and wild, sandy and pebbled
cons: Large size requires long driving times
Can get very crowded in Chalkida on weekends
Some of the most beautiful remote beaches require driving on dirt roads
insider_tips: Cross the bridge at Chalkida on a weekday to avoid major traffic
Explore Mount Dirfys and the Dimosari Gorge for the best nature experiences
Visit the seaside hot springs in Edipsos for a unique wellness experience
Take time to discover the remote, unnamed beaches in the south
Try local figs and mountain honey, they are exceptional
Pick one region (North, Central, or South) to explore deeply rather than rushing the whole island
mistakes_to_avoid: Rushing to see the whole island in two days—you'll spend your time in the car
Skipping the local mountain tavernas in favor of resort food
Visiting popular beaches on peak August weekends if you hate crowds
Underestimating the driving distances between regions
Assuming all beaches are easily accessible by sedan
